questions: You're someone who takes things at face value and generally assumes people have good intentions. Your boss schedules a surprise one-on-one meeting with no agenda. What's your first thought?
You're someone who takes things at face value and generally assumes people have good intentions. Two colleagues stop talking when you walk into the break room. What do you think just happened?

You're someone who takes things at face value and generally assumes people have good intentions. A friend you haven't heard from in years suddenly reaches out wanting to reconnect. What's your initial reaction?

You're someone who takes things at face value and generally assumes people have good intentions. Your partner's phone buzzes late at night and they turn it face-down without looking. What do you think?
You're someone who takes things at face value and generally assumes people have good intentions. A new security camera is installed near your workspace without announcement. What's your reaction?

You're someone who takes things at face value and generally assumes people have good intentions. You notice your neighbor watching from their window as you leave for work several mornings in a row. What do you think?

You're someone who takes things at face value and generally assumes people have good intentions. A coworker keeps asking detailed questions about your project timeline and deliverables. What's going through your mind?

You're someone who takes things at face value and generally assumes people have good intentions. You get a call from an unknown number right after submitting a job application somewhere. What do you assume?
You're someone who takes things at face value and generally assumes people have good intentions. Your company announces a new employee monitoring software to 'improve productivity.' How do you react?
You're someone who takes things at face value and generally assumes people have good intentions. A friend tags you in a social media post you didn't approve. What's your first thought?
